parsed_review.summary: CP00-159 advances the RP04 master-data closeout chain from CP00-158 by adding descriptor-only service review-path and integration-smoke evidence cases plus H04/C04 evidence and review-packet builders, with matching contract service_evidence block, pack binding, no-write attestation, and validator coverage. The diff is internally consistent: pack chain (CP158 upstream, CP160/RP04.P02.M09.S19 next), gates (H04/C04, claude-opus-4-8/max/read_only), production_ready_flag, public_exports, and unit/deliverable/micro-phase distributions all align across contract, registry, validators, tests, and the validation script. The no-write boundary holds (no IO, no route dispatch, no persistence, no Hermes/Claude execution) and the integration smoke test verifies hidden-field sanitization, descriptor-only routing, four distinct tail outcomes, and safe blocked-output codes. No P0/P1 issues found.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-159 builds service evidence and review packet descriptors only; package code does not execute Hermes or Claude and does not write product state.

findings.title: CP00-159 closeout manifest not yet written; closeout-pack:validate covers only through CP00-158
findings.evidence: Per the supplied evidence, closeout-pack:validate passes only through CP00-158 because CP00-159 closeout artifacts are not yet written. The package gracefully falls back: tests and validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs resolve cp159PlanPack from the closeout plan (which contains CP00-159 as queue head) when the manifest is missing, so all stated validations still pass. This is expected sequencing (review the code/contract diff, then finalize the closeout artifact), not a code defect in the diff.
findings.recommended_action: As the closing step, generate docs/closeout-packs/cp00-159/manifest.json and re-run closeout-pack:validate so it covers CP00-159 before final close.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: generated docs/closeout-packs/cp00-159 closeout artifacts and reran closeout-pack validation for CP00-159 before commit.

findings.title: validateMasterDataCp159ServiceEvidence is largely self-referential
findings.evidence: The function checks that MASTER_DATA_CP159_SERVICE_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENTS.required_service_evidence_fields contains each of an 18-entry list that is duplicated literally inside the same function, and asserts the attestation booleans. This will pass as long as the requirements constant matches the inlined literal; it does not verify that the generated descriptors/packets actually surface or exercise those 18 fields. Behavioral coverage of the fields comes from the smoke/review tests and the inherited CP157/158 descriptors, which is good, but the validator itself adds limited independent assurance.
findings.recommended_action: Optional: assert the 18 fields against the contract's service_tail/service_evidence projection (or against generated descriptor keys) rather than an inlined literal, to give real drift detection.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: validateMasterDataCp159ServiceEvidence now accepts a contract service_evidence projection and checks required fields/outcomes/no-write boundary against it; tests and script pass the contract projection.

findings.title: Hidden-field sanitization checks key on a literal sentinel string
findings.evidence: Both checks compute !JSON.stringify(tail).includes("must not leak"). They confirm the blocked-case test fixture's secret value is stripped, but they only detect that specific sentinel, not the general class of declared hidden_source_fields. The actual sanitization is performed by the CP00-158 createMasterDataServiceTailDescriptor and is already validated; these are smoke assertions, which is acceptable for their purpose.
findings.recommended_action: Optional: assert against the contract hidden_source_fields set (e.g., confirm none of those keys appear in serialized descriptor output) for a more general guarantee.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: integration smoke now injects per-hidden-field probe values and asserts all probe values are absent from serialized descriptors.

findings.title: CP159 no_write_attestation redundantly re-asserts fields already inherited from the CP158 spread
findings.evidence: The object spreads ...MASTER_DATA_CP158_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and then explicitly re-sets eight already-false keys (executes_hermes_command, executes_claude_review, sends_claude_prompt, dispatches_review_route, dispatches_approval_route, writes_persistence_boundary, executes_rollback, executes_retry). The explicit overrides read as intentional defensive documentation for the pack that builds (but never executes) Hermes/Claude packets. The side effect is that these eight values are now decoupled from CP158: if an upstream attestation value changed, the override would silently force it back, masking divergence. Relying on the stated rp04:master-data:validate pass, all eight keys are present in contract.no_write_attestation, so the validator loop is satisfied.
findings.recommended_action: Optional: drop the redundant overrides (rely on the spread) unless the explicitness is deliberate; if kept, treat it as intentional re-assertion.
findings.post_review_disposition: explicitly deferred as intentional defensive re-assertion: CP159 repeats inherited no-write booleans because it builds Hermes/Claude packet descriptors without executing either runtime path.

findings.title: Minor contract-validator coverage gaps (schema_version, service_evidence.no_write_boundary)
findings.evidence: schema_version was bumped v0.3 -> v0.4 (appropriate for the new service_evidence block) but the validator does not assert schema_version, and contract.service_evidence.no_write_boundary is present and consistent with the requirements constant but is not asserted (review_packet_mode is asserted). Drift in either field would not be caught. This is a pre-existing pattern, not introduced functionality.
findings.recommended_action: Optional: add requireEqual checks for schema_version and service_evidence.no_write_boundary.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: validate-rp04-master-data-contract now asserts schema_version and service_evidence.no_write_boundary.

parsed_review.p3_disposition: three P3 findings fixed; one P3 explicitly deferred as intentional defensive no-write re-assertion
